title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. heredia
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. heredia, also known as the bracken fern, is a subspecies of the bracken fern plant, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the lycopodiopsida class. The medicinal properties of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. heredia include its ability to reduce inflammation, act as an antioxidant, and inhibit the growth of cancer cells. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve immune function, and reduce the risk of certain types of cancer. Pteridium aquilinum subsp. heredia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
